codeReview.summary: Commit bee7f023 correctly removes sleep-based backoff from the number/order/invoice generators, strengthens the sales-alias contention proof, and wires the new order/invoice contention suites into the targeted accounting proof pack. However, the accounting-core replay rewrite is not safe under concurrent duplicate submissions: several reservation-first accounting writers now replace the former wait-for-leader window with a single point-in-time lookup, so a follower request that collides while the leader transaction is still open can throw INTERNAL_CONCURRENCY_FAILURE instead of replaying the reserved result. This review is orthogonal to the prior accounting-core decomposition failure from fix-remaining-accounting-scrutiny-gaps, which is neither addressed nor worsened here.

issues.description: The manual-journal idempotency path now does a single `findReservedJournalEntry(...)` read immediately after `reserveManualReference(...)` reports an existing reservation. The reserve-first comment at lines 3741-3744 makes clear the reservation row is created before the leader journal exists, so a concurrent duplicate request can legitimately hit lines 3752-3759 before the leader commits and now gets `INTERNAL_CONCURRENCY_FAILURE` instead of the prior replay behavior. That regresses concurrent idempotent manual-journal submissions.

The same single-read regression exists across the reservation-first receipt/payment/settlement/correction paths. `reserveReferenceMapping(...)` inserts the idempotency mapping before the leader writes journal/allocation rows (lines 4221-4266), but the non-leader branches now do one immediate `findReservedJournalEntry(...)` / `findReservedAllocations(...)` read and then throw if nothing is visible yet. For example, `recordDealerReceipt` now throws `missingReservedPartnerAllocation(...)` at line 1324 after one read, and the same pattern appears in `recordDealerReceiptSplit` (1529-1543), `recordSupplierPayment` (2279-2302), `settleDealerInvoices` (2554-2580), `settleSupplierInvoices` (2829-2855), and the credit/debit-note replay branches (6698-6799). Under normal concurrent duplicates, the loser can now fail even though the leader will commit successfully moments later.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The canonical `targeted-accounting-proof` pack was updated for order/invoice contention, but there is still no direct duplicate-replay contention probe for the accounting reserved-row paths that this packet rewrote.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: bee7f023 changes `AccountingCoreEngineCore` replay branches for manual journals and receipt/payment/settlement flows, while `.factory/services.yaml` only swaps `InvoiceNumberServiceTest` for `OrderNumberServiceContentionIT` and `InvoiceNumberServiceContentionIT`, and the packet adds only those two new contention tests plus the updated `CriticalAccountingAxesIT` sales-alias scenario.

summary: FAIL: the feature removes sleep-based waits and adds the requested generator/alias contention coverage, but the new single-read accounting replay logic can now surface `INTERNAL_CONCURRENCY_FAILURE` for normal concurrent duplicate accounting submissions instead of replaying the leader result.
